# Wickra Copilot

> A local market copilot grounded in real order-book, liquidation and funding microstructure. It derives structured facts from live microstructure with the Wickra core, then answers questions about them through any LLM (or none) — LLM-agnostic and offline-first. The fact-derivation core is deterministic and byte-identical across all ten language bindings.

Wickra Copilot separates grounding from generation: a Rust core folds market microstructure into a structured set of facts (order-book state, liquidation and funding context, derived indicators), exposed as a JSON-over-C-ABI data API in Rust, Python, Node.js, WASM and — over a C ABI — C, C++, C#, Go, Java and R. A small LLM layer (four provider presets plus custom, all optional) turns those grounded facts into answers, so the copilot never hallucinates the market state. Released under the MIT OR Apache-2.0 license.

## Source and packages
- [GitHub](https://github.com/wickra-lib/wickra-copilot): Source, issues, and releases.
- [crates.io](https://crates.io/crates/wickra-copilot): Rust crate.
- [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/wickra-copilot/): Python package.
- [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/wickra-copilot): Node package.

## Ecosystem
- [Wickra](https://wickra.org): The core streaming-indicator library the copilot is built on.
- [Docs](https://docs.wickra.org): Documentation for the Wickra core.
